name: Complejo Lagunar de Alcázar de San Juan — circular desde Alcázar de San Juan
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alcázar De San Juan traverse the flat plains of La Mancha, offering expansive views and a sense of open freedom. The region's landscape incorporates rivers, lagoons, and gentle hills, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Iconic features like the historic windmills of Campo de Criptana serve as prominent backdrops for many routes. This area combines cultural heritage with natural beauty, making it suitable for diverse cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Alcázar De San Juan The most popular touring cycling route is Complejo Lagunar de Alcázar de San Juan — circular desde Alcázar de San Juan, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the local lagoon complex, offering flat and accessible paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Campo de Criptana Windmills – Ermita de San Antón loop from Alcázar de San Juan, a difficult 64.3 miles (103.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and passes by the famous windmills of Campo de Criptana. Local touring cyclists also love the Río Záncara – Alcazár de San Juan loop from Alcázar de San Juan, a 49.3 miles (79.3 km) trail leading through the Río Záncara area, often completed in about 6 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcázar De San Juan is defined by the open La Mancha plains, historic windmills, and riverine landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Alcázar De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcázar De San Juan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcázar De San Juan?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes in the Alcázar De San Juan area. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 6 easy, 21 moderate, and 17 difficult options.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while cycling in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: You'll primarily experience the expansive, flat plains of La Mancha, offering wide-open views. The landscape also features rivers, lagoons, and gentle hills, providing varied terrain. A prominent feature you'll often see are the historic windmills of Campo de Criptana, adding a unique cultural backdrop to your ride.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 6 easy touring cycling routes around Alcázar De San Juan. A great option for an accessible ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057523497/complejo-lagunar-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-circular-desde-alcazar-de-san-juan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Complejo Lagunar de Alcázar de San Juan — circular desde Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;. This 14.6-mile (23.5 km) route explores the local lagoon complex on flat, accessible paths, making it ideal for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 17 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Criptana Windmills – Ermita de San Antón loop from Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 64.3 miles (103.5 km) with significant elevation gain, passing by the famous windmills. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Záncara – Alcazár de San Juan loop from Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, a 49.3-mile (79.3 km) trail through the Río Záncara area.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057523497/complejo-lagunar-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-circular-desde-alcazar-de-san-juan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Complejo Lagunar de Alcázar de San Juan — circular desde Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route exploring the local lagoons. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar de San Juan – Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 26.8-mile (43.1 km) ride past the iconic windmills.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural and historical sites. You can explore several lagoons, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Peñahueca&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic windmills of Campo de Criptana are a major highlight, and you might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3042061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinera Quarry of Piédrola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: The touring cycling routes around Alcázar De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the open La Mancha plains, the picturesque windmills, and the varied terrain that combines flat sections with gentle hills and riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there routes that specifically feature the Campo de Criptana Windmills?
A: Yes, the famous Campo de Criptana Windmills are a central feature of several routes. Besides the longer challenging loops, you can also find a shorter, difficult route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Criptana Windmills – Alcazár de San Juan loop from Alcázar de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18 miles (29 km) long and offers stunning views of these iconic structures.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcázar De San Juan?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot on the open plains of La Mancha, so if cycling during this season, it's advisable to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat and ensure you carry plenty of water.

Q: What are the typical durations for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes, like the 14.6-mile (23.5 km) lagoon loop, can take around 2 hours. Moderate routes might range from 3-5 hours, while difficult routes, such as the 64.3-mile (103.5 km) windmill loop, can take over 8 hours to complete.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on these routes?
A: The elevation gains vary with the route's difficulty. Easy routes, like the lagoon loop, have minimal elevation gain, around 250 feet (75 meters). More challenging routes, especially those traversing hills or longer distances, can feature significant climbs, with some difficult routes accumulating over 2,700 feet (830 meters) of ascent.
tips:
La Veguilla Lagoon belongs to the San Juan Lagoon Complex. Which is formed by two more lagoons, the Camino de Villafranca lagoon and the Yeguas lagoon.

These La Mancha wetlands are home to migratory birds that travel from Europe to Africa. In addition, they have several strategic points where the bird observatories are located.
It is part of the Alcázar de San Juan Lagoon Complex. There is a colony of flamingos and numerous ducks.

Alcázar de San Juan is one of the most interesting destinations in the heart of La Mancha, with a blend of history, heritage, nature, and Cervantine traditions.

Its name comes from the Military Order of Saint John, which dominated the area after the Reconquista.
It claims to be the birthplace of Miguel de Cervantes, as the baptismal font where, according to some studies, the writer was baptized is preserved in the Collegiate Church of Santa María la Mayor.
It was an important railway junction and economic center in the 19th and 20th centuries, which shaped its urban development.
